name: Bill Haley
description: '''William John Clifton Haley''' ({{IPAc-en|ˈ|h|eɪ|l|i}}; July 6, 1925 – February 9, 1981) was an American [[rock and roll]] musician. He is credited by many with first popularizing this form of music in the early 1950s with his group [[Bill Haley & His Comets]] and million-selling hits such as "[[Rock Around the Clock]]", "[[See You Later, Alligator]]", "[[Shake, Rattle and Roll]]", "[[Rocket 88]]", "[[Skinny Minnie]]", and "Razzle Dazzle". Haley has sold over 60 million records worldwide. In 1987, he was posthumously inducted into the [[Rock and Roll Hall of Fame]].
